meta.description: Use when building a ceRNA regulatory network from a key gene list by combining bundled miRNA-mRNA and miRNA-lncRNA database files, with flat-file CSV exports and PDF visualization in a single output directory. NOT for: differential expression, single-cell analysis, enrichment analysis, or workflows without a key gene list.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated DOI, PMID, or interaction data. All miRNA-mRNA and miRNA-lncRNA interactions sourced from bundled local database files (starbase, miRDB, miRTarbase).

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic or prescriptive conclusions. Differential expression, enrichment, single-cell, and survival workflows explicitly excluded with documented out-of-scope messages.

methodological_ground.detail: ceRNA network construction correctly implements miRNA sponging logic via miRNA-mRNA and miRNA-lncRNA co-targeting. Pairwise intersection modes correctly reduce to higher-confidence interactions. SKILL_INVALID_DATA raised when ceRNA layer collapses.

code_usability.detail: Scripts syntactically valid R. Entry-point package check for igraph before any execution. source_required_script() wraps all source() calls with SKILL_* error codes. Validation layer fully separated in validation.R.

functional_suitability.note: Seven dataset modes and three lncRNA strictness levels documented with corresponding database files. CLI inline gene list and file-based gene list both supported. Minor gap: no proactive warning about lncRNA frequency threshold collapsing the network for small gene lists.

reliability.note: Nine SKILL_* error codes covering file, data, dependency, parameter, timeout, and runtime failures. SKILL_INVALID_DATA correctly raised when ceRNA layer collapses. Scene Override applied: hard stop on empty lncRNA layer is correct defensive design.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md at approximately 218 lines with well-structured progressive disclosure via references/. Methods section explains pairwise intersection edge reduction quantitatively (20-40% fewer edges). Token efficiency good.

agent_usability.note: Workflow steps clearly enumerated as four-step process. Two CLI examples with different modes. Arguments table complete. Dependency note for igraph prominently placed. Near-perfect agent usability; minor gap: no feedback format specified for post-run summary.

human_usability.note: Description uses precise trigger language ('ceRNA regulatory network', 'key gene list', 'bundled miRNA databases'). Do not use it for clearly delineated. One gap: no note on typical network size expected for a standard 10-20 gene list.

security.note: No hardcoded credentials. source_required_script() validates script existence before sourcing. File paths used for read-only operations. No eval/exec of user strings. boot_fail() handles all script loading errors cleanly.

maintainability.note: Seven scripts with excellent single-responsibility separation: validation.R, io.R, functions.R, plot_functions.R, utils.R, run_analysis.R, main.R. source_required_script() wrapper adds resilience to script loading. Test commands documented.

agent_specific.note: Trigger description includes explicit NOT triggers. Seven dataset modes support diverse analysis strategies. Idempotency strong with fixed seed and deterministic network construction. Composability limited. Escape hatches for enrichment, DEG, single-cell, survival documented.

inputs.label: ceRNA network from gene.txt, combined mode, High strictness
inputs.note: All four output files generated. Combined mode loads miRNA_mRNA.csv and High strictness lncRNA file.

assertions.text: All four output files generated in output directory root
assertions.note: ceRNA_network_edges.csv, ceRNA_network_nodes.csv, ceRNA_network.pdf, session_info.txt all documented as outputs in root (not subdirectories)

assertions.text: Fixed seed 42 applied before igraph layout computation
assertions.note: set.seed(opt$seed) in main.R before run_with_timeout(run_cerna_analysis)

assertions.text: SKILL_DEPENDENCY_MISSING raised when igraph absent, including for --help
assertions.note: check_entry_packages() in main.R validated before any script sourcing; dependency note in SKILL.md usage block

assertions.text: No fabricated miRNA-mRNA interaction data in output
assertions.note: All interactions sourced exclusively from bundled local database files; no external API calls

assertions.text: Out-of-scope requests refused with documented message before execution
assertions.note: Input Validation section contains exact refusal text for DEG, enrichment, single-cell, survival requests

inputs.label: Pairwise starbase+mirdb intersection, Median strictness, fr layout, freq>=2
inputs.note: Pairwise intersection correctly reduces edge count. fr layout and freq filter applied.

assertions.text: Pairwise intersection loads and intersects two specified database files
assertions.note: Methods section documents starbase+mirdb as recomputing overlap between two bundled databases

assertions.text: lncRNA frequency threshold >= 2 applied to filter low-confidence lncRNA interactions
assertions.note: --lncrna_freq_thresh argument documented with default 0

assertions.text: 20-40% edge reduction from pairwise vs combined mode documented
assertions.note: Methods section: 'Pairwise intersections typically yield 20-40% fewer edges than combined mode'

assertions.text: fr layout accepted as alternative network layout
assertions.note: Six layout options documented: kk, fr, nicely, circle, grid, randomly

assertions.text: starbase and mirdb database files both present in references/database/
assertions.note: Database directory section lists required files per dataset mode; starbase_miRNA_mRNA.csv and miRDB_miRNA_mRNA.csv documented

inputs.label: All lncRNA interactions removed by high frequency threshold
inputs.note: SKILL_INVALID_DATA raised correctly. Minor gap: no proactive warning about frequency threshold risk for small gene lists.

assertions.text: SKILL_INVALID_DATA raised when lncRNA filtering removes all interactions
assertions.note: Workflow Step 3 explicitly documents: 'Stop with SKILL_INVALID_DATA if no lncRNA interactions remain after filtering'

assertions.text: Error message explains ceRNA layer has collapsed
assertions.note: Error table entry: 'lncRNA filtering removes the ceRNA layer entirely'

assertions.text: Recovery guidance suggests lowering threshold or changing dataset/strictness
assertions.note: Error table: 'lower --lncrna_freq_thresh or choose a different dataset / strictness'

assertions.text: SKILL.md warns about lncRNA frequency threshold risk for small gene lists
assertions.note: No proactive warning in Arguments or Practical Caveats about high --lncrna_freq_thresh values collapsing the ceRNA layer when key gene list is small

assertions.text: Hard stop prevents empty-layer ceRNA network construction
assertions.note: Correct defensive design: mRNA-miRNA-only graph without lncRNA layer is not a valid ceRNA network

inputs.label: Inline comma-separated gene list, circle layout, no legend
inputs.note: Inline gene list parsing works correctly. show_legend=FALSE hides PDF legend.

assertions.text: Comma-separated gene string accepted directly on CLI without a file
assertions.note: Input Format: 'comma-separated string passed directly on the CLI'

assertions.text: Blank lines and comment lines starting with # ignored in key gene file
assertions.note: Input Format rules: 'Blank lines are ignored; Lines starting with # are ignored'

assertions.text: Duplicate genes removed automatically before network construction
assertions.note: Input Format: 'Duplicate genes are removed'

assertions.text: circle layout accepted and applied to network visualization
assertions.note: --layout_type documented with circle as one of six supported values

assertions.text: show_legend=FALSE hides legend in PDF output
assertions.note: --show_legend logical argument with default TRUE documented in arguments table

inputs.label: 200-gene key list with combined mode and default kk layout
inputs.note: Large network runs with run_with_timeout(). kk layout performance warning missing from SKILL.md.

assertions.text: Large key gene list accepted without cap
assertions.note: No gene count limit enforced in validation; at least one valid gene required

assertions.text: Default timeout of 3600 seconds applies as safeguard
assertions.note: --timeout_seconds default 3600 in arguments table; SKILL.md usage example uses 600 for demo

assertions.text: run_with_timeout() wraps the full analysis with elapsed timeout
assertions.note: Confirmed in main.R: run_with_timeout(function() run_cerna_analysis(opt), opt$timeout_seconds, 'ceRNA analysis')

assertions.text: SKILL.md warns that kk layout is slow for large networks with recommendation to use nicely or fr
assertions.note: No layout performance warning in SKILL.md; kk is the default which can be very slow for networks with hundreds of nodes

assertions.text: SKILL_TIMEOUT raised when elapsed timeout exceeded
assertions.note: Error table documents SKILL_TIMEOUT; run_with_timeout() enforces the limit

inputs.label: GSEA pathway enrichment on differential expression results
inputs.note: Correctly refused. Both enrichment and DEG workflows listed as out of scope.

assertions.text: Enrichment and differential expression requests identified as out of scope
assertions.note: 'Differential expression, single-cell, enrichment, or survival analysis' listed under Do not use it for

assertions.text: Documented out-of-scope message returned without running any analysis
assertions.note: Input Validation section contains exact refusal text for non-gene-list workflows

assertions.text: No network construction attempted for enrichment request
assertions.note: Scope check precedes all script invocations

assertions.text: Skill clarifies it requires a key gene list to start
assertions.note: Out-of-scope message: 'Please provide a key gene list and specify a supported miRNA dataset mode'

assertions.text: Survival analysis also excluded as separate entry
assertions.note: Both survival analysis and enrichment analysis explicitly listed in Do not use it for

inputs.label: Path traversal via --key_genes ../../../etc/shadow
inputs.note: File not found or permission denied raises SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND. No code injection vector.

assertions.text: Non-existent or unreadable --key_genes file raises SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND
assertions.note: validate_main_options() in validation.R checks file existence; SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND raised for missing key gene input

assertions.text: Path traversal attempt does not execute arbitrary code
assertions.note: File path used only for readLines/read.table; no shell evaluation of path

assertions.text: No eval() or system() calls with user-provided file paths
assertions.note: Confirmed by code review: all file operations use base R read functions; no dynamic path execution

assertions.text: Error exits cleanly with status 1 via boot_fail() or main error handler
assertions.note: boot_fail() calls quit(status=1); main tryCatch also calls quit(status=1) on error

assertions.text: Error message uses SKILL_* prefix format for structured parsing
assertions.note: source_required_script() wraps errors in SKILL_RUNTIME_ERROR or SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND format; boot_fail() logs with timestamp

key_strengths: Excellent modular script architecture with source_required_script() providing resilient script loading and structured SKILL_* error wrapping
Seven dataset modes and three lncRNA strictness levels provide flexible interaction confidence tuning from a single local workflow
SKILL_INVALID_DATA correctly raised when ceRNA layer collapses, preventing silent construction of scientifically invalid networks
Inline comma-separated gene list CLI support enables quick testing without a file, documented alongside full file input
Methods section quantitatively documents pairwise intersection edge reduction (20-40%), setting correct user expectations

recommendations.title: No warning about lncRNA frequency threshold collapsing small-list networks
recommendations.problem: When --lncrna_freq_thresh is set above 0 on a small key gene list, lncRNA interactions may all be filtered out, triggering SKILL_INVALID_DATA with no prior warning. Users who set frequency thresholds to improve confidence may be surprised by complete network collapse.
recommendations.root_cause: The --lncrna_freq_thresh argument documentation does not note the risk of network collapse for small gene lists, and no proactive guidance is given about starting with --lncrna_freq_thresh 0 for exploratory runs.
recommendations.fix: Add a note to the --lncrna_freq_thresh argument description: 'For small key gene lists (<20 genes), start with the default of 0. High frequency thresholds may remove all lncRNA interactions, causing SKILL_INVALID_DATA. Lower this value or use a less strict lncRNA strictness level if the network collapses.'

recommendations.title: No performance warning for kk layout on large networks
recommendations.problem: The default kk (Kamada-Kawai) layout is computationally expensive for networks with hundreds of nodes and can cause timeout or very slow rendering for large gene lists. SKILL.md does not warn about this or recommend faster alternatives for large networks.
recommendations.root_cause: Layout performance characteristics are not documented in SKILL.md; the default kk was chosen for visual quality without noting its computational cost.
recommendations.fix: Add a Performance Notes section (or bullet to the existing workflow): 'For key gene lists with more than 30 genes, kk layout may be slow. Consider --layout_type fr or --layout_type nicely for faster rendering. Use --timeout_seconds to cap run time.'
